Advisor to Iran's Leader Dr. Ali Akbar Velayati said that US President Donald Trump’s “foolery and theatrical behavior” cannot conceal the broader geopolitical realities taking shape. He pointed to the withdrawal of US troops from Germany, the weakening of NATO, and repeated technical issues affecting American warships as signs that the White House’s illusions are collapsing.

Velayati also criticized Trump for threatening Iran with famine, arguing that global food security and chemical fertilizer supply chains are heavily linked to routes passing through the Strait of Hormuz, which he said demonstrates Washington’s lack of understanding of the global economic and political landscape.

Addressing Trump directly, Velayati said that international politics is not like the world of movies or fictional characters like Jack Sparrow, warning that anyone who “plays with the lifeline of the world” risks placing themselves in a deadlock.

#Iran #WarOnIran #StraitOfHormuz #Trump #UnitedStates #AlMayadeen

In an interview with Al Mayadeen, former Pentagon official Michael Maloof said that US President Donald Trump is facing “very tight deadlines.”

He explained that Trump is bound by the 60-day deadline to report to Congress, stressing that the claim that a ceasefire “stopped the clock” does not hold under the law. Maloof added that Trump did not seek congressional approval to continue the war against Iran, which is legally required.

The former Pentagon official also noted that Congress must approve Trump’s $200 billion supplemental request to continue the war, arguing it will be difficult to pass “because he has flaunted the law.”

He added that Trump is also under pressure from another deadline: his upcoming trip to China.

#AlMayadeen #Trump #China #IranWar

Mojtaba Zarei, a member of Iran’s parliamentary National Security and Foreign Policy Committee, said he met Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf on Saturday. 

Zarei said they both discussed Trump's “cognitive warfare following the US' defeat, as well as the continuation of the war through the Islamabad talks."

Zarei said he decided to share the details of his meeting with the public and “truth-seeking elites,” while respecting legal and classification limits.

He stated that the Islamabad talks, according to Ghalibaf, were conducted with the approval of the Leader, Sayyed Mojtaba Khamenei, stressing that no negotiations could take place, religiously or legally, without his permission.

According to Zarei, who cited Ghalibaf, both the authorization and the substance of the talks followed the Leader’s directives. When the US side attempted to move into detailed nuclear discussions, the Iranian delegation said it was prohibited from proceeding into detailed talks on this issue, adding that decisions on this issue rest solely with the Leader.

Zarei also said that the Lebanese Resistance and people were a condition for entering a ceasefire phase. He added that this was not only the Leader’s directive but a point of consensus, noting that President Pezeshkian rejected any attempt to separate the Resistance, which Pezeshkian said would have been "dishonorable and unjust."

#Iran #IranWar #Lebanon #WestAsia
